data.body:
DEEP-RESEARCH RESULT (wf_3b8affe5-14b; 108 agents, 24/25 claims confirmed) — convergent with the
first-turn survey. Applied the new "interrogate novelty" lens.

VERDICT: nothing off-the-shelf satisfies all three of {git-native TEXT as single source of truth +
bidirectional visual editor + mixes LLM and deterministic steps}. Recommendation: KEEP the homegrown
YAML + thin-executor engine; the visual layer is a SEPARABLE, deferrable concern.

EVIDENCE (confirmed):
- Langflow (147K+ stars): DB-canonical (SQLite default); JSON is export/interchange, NOT source of
  truth; co-mingles layout (node x/y + viewport zoom) with semantics -> noisy diffs. Mixed LLM+code
  (Python per component). Adopting it breaks the git-text invariant.
- n8n: the cautionary FAILURE MODE — JSON co-mingles layout/IDs/timestamps; git pull does NOT 3-way
  merge ("Pull and override" loses local edits); vendor visual-diff UI exists to paper over noisy JSON;
  fair-code (Sustainable Use License), not OSI-open.
- CWL: TRUE text-canonical YAML/JSON, dynamic fan-out via scatter/gather — proves text-canonical + map
  is viable — BUT read-only viewer only (view.commonwl.org) and NO agentic/LLM-step concept.
- GitHub-Actions visual editors are the cleanest existence proof of "text-as-source + visual
  projection": JetBrains plugin keeps layout in a SIDECAR (.idea/githubActionsLayout.xml), YAML stays
  clean; timoa/workflow-editor serializes back to the authoritative YAML. BUT GHA-only, no LLM steps,
  immature (~11 stars), and timoa's round-trip is structural-not-byte-lossless (drops comments/
  whitespace -> first-save diff noise). [A "real-time bidirectional sync" sub-claim was REFUTED 0-3.]
- n8n-as-code (~1.4k stars): third-party inversion to .workflow.ts "easier for people and AGENTS to
  edit", repo (not DB) is source of truth, git-like push/pull w/ 3-way conflict resolution — proves
  agent-editability + source-of-truth inversion are achievable; still co-mingles layout.
- 2026 emerging (surfaced, lightly verified): Microsoft Conductor (deterministic orchestration for
  multi-agent AI workflows, May 2026), arXiv 2510.04173, "Open Agent Spec 2026" (blog).

NOVELTY ANALYSIS (per the new skill principle): our architecture is NOT novel as architecture — CWL +
the GHA editors validate "git-text source-of-truth + thin executor + visual-as-projection" for
deterministic workflows. It is novel ONLY in the combination {mixed LLM+deterministic + a bidirectional
editor}. WHY is that gap there? BENIGN reason: the popular AI-workflow tools (Langflow, n8n) are hosted
SaaS products for non-technical users — they need real-time multi-user collab, persistent runtime
state, and a polished canvas, which make a DATABASE the natural source of truth. Git-cleanliness and
agent-editing are simply not their constraints. So the absence is "the market optimizes for a different
user (humans in a SaaS) than rebar (agents in a git repo)", NOT "people tried git-text-canonical for AI
workflows and it failed." Green-flag novelty (a real gap from a different constraint set), with ONE real
remaining landmine: clean diffs require keeping layout OUT of the semantic text (sidecar OR
layout-free + deterministic auto-layout) AND comment/whitespace-preserving (byte-stable) serialization
if an editor ever round-trips.

CAVEATS (research-flagged gaps): THREAD 2 broad format survey (BPMN/Argo/Dify/Kestra/Flyte/Temporal/
Prefect/Dagster/Node-RED/2026 specs) largely UNVERIFIED in this pass (some covered by the first-turn
survey); THREAD 4 first-person "why DB-canonical" rationale is INFERRED, not directly sourced.
